title: Faculty of Humanities and Social Sciences Administrative Staff
content:
Faculty of Humanities and Social Sciences Administrative Staff

Burcu Ceylan

has been working as Faculty Secretary since 2021. Ceylan is a graduate of Üsküdar University, Department of Sociology. Burcu Ceylan's trainings and certificate information are as follows:

YÖK Legislation Seminars and Meetings

University Management System Training

Official Correspondence Rules Training

Document and Archive Management

Leadership and Management Trainings

Personnel and Student Affairs Management

Information Systems and Office Programs Training (Word, Excel, PowerPoint etc.), Advanced Excel Training

Orientation activities

Occupational Health and Safety

Ayberk Paçacı

has been working at the Faculty of Humanities and Social Sciences since 2020. Working as a Registrar and Archive Specialist, Paçacı graduated from Üsküdar University, Faculty of Health Sciences, Department of Social Work. Ayber Paçacı's trainings and certificate information are as follows:

Occupational Health and Safety

Document and Archive Management

In addition to these administrative staff, the Erasmus Office, Career Center, Data Processing Center, Student Affairs Unit Department of Üsküdar University are also working with the Erasmus Office, Career Center, Data Processing Center, Student Affairs Unit Department.The Faculty of Humanities and Social Sciences is in close cooperation with our faculty and provides assistance and support in various areas for the activities carried out within the Faculty.

In order to provide service at the Faculty of Humanities and Social Sciences, 7 security personnel work in shifts so as not to be idle for 24 hours, supported by 2 technical personnel, 4 BSA cleaning and 1 tea service personnel. These personnel receive Occupational Health and Safety Training and On-the-Job Training to take part in the service. The assigned administrative and technical staff assist students and faculty members when infrastructure and administrative problems are encountered so that the courses on the Campus run smoothly and students can receive uninterrupted education. Administrative and technical staff have the quantity and qualifications appropriate to the program learning outcomes.
